experiment: B23: B2 unchanged + Fourier Random Amplitude Mixup training
note: B23 keeps the exact B2 network and losses. The only change is applying low-frequency Fourier amplitude mixup during training to simulate radiograph exposure/contrast/style shifts while preserving phase-driven anatomical structure and the original mask label.

result_reflection.b18: EMA reduced loss and almost matched B2, implying the base model is strong but may need better style-level generalization
result_reflection.b20: distance supervision was dominated by near-zero background and did not improve lesion recall
result_reflection.b21: feature enhancement branch learned no useful scale
result_reflection.design_implication: avoid more branches; improve robustness of the already-good B2 decision boundary through realistic X-ray style perturbation

design_references.title: Generalizable Medical Image Segmentation via Random Amplitude Mixup and Domain-Specific Image Restoration
design_references.adopted_idea: Fourier amplitude perturbation can improve robustness to acquisition/style variation

design_references.title: When CNNs Outperform Transformers and Mambas: Revisiting Deep Architectures for Dental Caries Segmentation
design_references.adopted_idea: dental caries radiograph segmentation benefits from strong CNN baselines and careful validation more than unnecessary architectural complexity

ram_lite.position: training batch only, before forward pass
ram_lite.amplitude: low-frequency amplitude softly mixed with another batch image
